text.title: Metric Selection for Solopreneurs
text.summary: Metric categories, the rule for choosing the right metrics, and an essential metrics table for solo operators.

rule.statement: Track at most 10 metrics at any time; include exactly one north star metric, two to three leading indicators, and two to three lagging measures of financial health.
rule.rationale: Tracking too many metrics produces analysis paralysis and dilutes ownership. Fewer metrics with clear owners and targets produce faster decisions and clearer accountability.

rule.statement: Prefer actionable metrics (metrics you can change with a specific intervention) over vanity metrics (metrics that look good but don't inform decisions).
rule.rationale: Vanity metrics (total sign-ups all-time, page views) feel good to track but provide no decision signal. Actionable metrics (CAC by channel this month, churn rate) point to specific interventions.

section.title: Metric Categories
section.p: Five categories: North Star (ultimate success measure — active users or revenue), Leading (predict future outcomes — sign-ups, trial starts), Lagging (measure past results — revenue, churn), Input (actions you control — emails sent, ad spend), Output (results you want — conversions, revenue).

section.title: Essential Solopreneur Metrics
section.p: Minimum viable set with review frequency: Revenue (daily), MRR/ARR (weekly), active paying customers (weekly), churn rate (monthly), CAC (monthly), LTV (monthly), profit margin (monthly), runway (monthly). Do not add metrics beyond this set until the business has a dedicated operator for each new metric.
